campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding — Your Policy, Explained
campaigns.objective: Make new policyholders feel they made the right call before they ever file a claim — teach them how cashless treatment, pre-authorisation and the hospital network actually work, so the value is felt long before the first claim event.
campaigns.trigger: Policy purchased + digital policy document issued (signup / account created). Email 1 sends within seconds of payment.
campaigns.segment: New policyholders. Branch by plan (Standard vs Platinum) and by who is covered (individual vs family floater vs senior parents).
campaigns.rationale: Acko's definitive 'aha' — a cashless claim approved in hours — may be months or years away. The onboarding flow bridges policy-in-hand to claim-confidence by making the customer feel the value now. The #1 objection ('will they actually pay?') doesn't disappear at purchase; it must be answered with the 98% settlement story before a claim ever happens. This is the fastest activation win and the foundation for renewal.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, confirm the policy is live, deliver the instant 'aha' — your digital policy is already in your inbox, no agent, no courier, no wait.
emails.subject: You're covered, [First name] — your policy is already done
emails.preview: No agent call, no paperwork, no waiting. Here's your policy document.
keyPoints: Confirm coverage is active from today — sum insured, plan, and who's covered, in plain English
One job today: save the policy PDF and the 24/7 claims number to your phone
Reassure: zero co-pay, zero room-rent sub-limits, zero GST — exactly what you signed up for

emails.purpose: Show the core value: how cashless treatment works at 11,500+ hospitals, so they know what to do before a crisis, not during one.
emails.subject: If you're ever hospitalised, here's exactly what happens
emails.preview: Cashless at 11,500+ hospitals — no arranging funds, no forms at discharge.
keyPoints: Walk through cashless in 3 steps: show your policy at the network hospital desk, we pre-authorise, you pay nothing
Bookmark the hospital locator now so it's ready before you need it
Contrast with the old way: no arranging lakhs in advance, no reimbursement paperwork

emails.purpose: Handle the #1 objection — trust — with social proof and the settlement story.
emails.subject: "Will they actually pay?" — here's our honest answer
emails.preview: 98% of claims settled, ₹400+ crore paid. Real customers, real bills.
keyPoints: Address the trust question head-on: 98% claims settlement rate (IRDAI-compliant framing)
Real, named customer claim experiences — 'they paid the full bill, all cashless'
Explain pre-authorisation desk and 24/7 support — assisted exactly when it counts

emails.purpose: Education — the mistakes that void or shrink other people's claims, and how Acko's structure avoids them.
emails.subject: The waiting-period trap most people find out about too late
emails.preview: Sub-limits, co-pays, disease waiting periods — what to check in any policy.
keyPoints: Educate on waiting periods, co-pays and room-rent caps — the fine print that guts most policies
Show how their Acko plan handles each (zero co-pay; Platinum zero waiting period)
If on Standard with parents/family, soft note that Platinum removes the waiting period

emails.purpose: Soft mid-onboarding check-in with a real human reply path; nudge to complete the free check-up if eligible.
emails.subject: Quick question — is anything about your cover unclear?
emails.preview: Hit reply, a real person reads it. Plus: claim your free health check-up.
keyPoints: Invite eligible members to claim the free medical check-up sweetener
Genuine reply-to: ask if anything about coverage or claims is unclear
Point to the app for digital ID card, network hospitals, and one-tap claim initiation

campaigns.name: Quote → Purchase Conversion (Premium Calculator Nurture)
campaigns.objective: Convert premium-calculator visitors who got a quote but didn't buy, by dismantling the trust objection over a short considered cycle and ending on a clear, low-friction close.
campaigns.trigger: Premium calculator used / quote generated, no policy purchased within 24 hours. Lead score climbs on repeat plan-page visits and comparison views.
campaigns.segment: Quote leads below the purchase threshold. Branch by quoted plan tier and by segment (young professional, young family, senior parents). Route high-intent (returned to pricing 2×) to an accelerated close.
campaigns.rationale: Acko's audience browses, compares, and returns — and the calculator is the main no-commitment entry point. The single biggest barrier is 'can I trust a newer insurer to pay?'. A structured multi-touch nurture that leads with trust and proof (not discounts) is exactly what's missing today, where leads likely get one or two generic follow-ups. The ZERO-GST 18% saving and free check-up are the close, not the opener.

emails.purpose: Deliver/re-surface their saved quote and frame the real problem: a hospital bill you can't predict.
emails.subject: [First name], your Acko quote is saved — ₹18/day to never see a surprise bill
emails.preview: Your quote is ready whenever you are. No agent will call you.
emails.cta: Pick up where you left off
keyPoints: Re-surface their exact quote — plan, sum insured, monthly premium
Frame the problem the quote solves: an unplanned hospital bill that derails savings
Reassure that buying is fully self-serve, minutes online, no agent, no pressure

emails.purpose: Answer the #1 objection — trust — before price.
emails.subject: Newer insurer. Older question: will Acko actually pay?
emails.preview: 98% of claims settled, ₹400+ crore paid out, mostly cashless.
keyPoints: Meet the trust objection directly — 98% settlement, ₹400+ crore paid (IRDAI-compliant)
Cashless at 11,500+ hospitals — you don't arrange funds during the emergency
Position 'people before paperwork': no middleman commission inflating your premium

emails.purpose: Plan comparison story — help them choose Standard vs Platinum for their situation.
emails.subject: Standard or Platinum? Here's how to choose in 2 minutes
emails.preview: Zero waiting period vs lower premium — which one fits you.
keyPoints: Side-by-side: Standard (lower premium) vs Platinum (zero waiting period, 10% inflation protection, higher room cover)
Match to segment: young single buyer vs family floater vs senior parents
Re-anchor on zero co-pay and zero room-rent sub-limits in both

emails.purpose: Social proof — real claim testimonials handling the 'when it's my turn' fear.
emails.subject: "It was all cashless. I didn't have to arrange a single rupee."
emails.preview: Real Acko customers, real hospital bills, paid in full.
keyPoints: Lead with a real customer quote in their own words
Show a cashless claim timeline — admission to approval in hours
Reinforce 24/7 support and pre-authorisation desk for the moment it matters

emails.purpose: Clear, low-friction close with the genuine value levers — ZERO GST saving and free check-up.
emails.subject: Save 18% instantly — ZERO GST on your health plan
emails.preview: Your quote, ZERO GST, and a free check-up. Done in minutes online.
keyPoints: Lead the offer: ZERO GST = 18% instant saving vs typical health premiums
Free medical check-up for eligible applicants as the post-decision sweetener
One-tap path from saved quote to instant digital policy — no agent, no paperwork

campaigns.name: Renewal, Anniversary & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect year-1 renewal revenue against price-poaching competitors by making the customer feel the premium was worth it — even if they never claimed — and grow accounts via upgrade and family additions.
campaigns.trigger: Renewal date − 60 days; policy anniversary; sum-insured/add-on page visit signalling expansion readiness. Branch by claim history and plan health.
campaigns.segment: Active policyholders approaching renewal. High-value branch: no-claim customers questioning value (save + reframe). Expansion branch: healthy multi-year customers (upgrade / add family / increase cover).
campaigns.rationale: Annual renewal is Acko's single biggest danger window — competitors actively target policyholders with cheaper base quotes, and no-claim customers wonder 'I paid ₹8,000 and never used it.' Today renewal is likely a price-only invoice. A value-recap narrative plus an inflation-protection upgrade story is the highest revenue-protection play in the portfolio.

emails.purpose: Value recap — reframe a no-claim year as a win, not wasted money.
emails.subject: A whole year covered, [First name] — here's what that really bought you
emails.preview: No claim? That's the best outcome. Here's the protection you carried all year.
keyPoints: Reframe: a claim-free year means you stayed healthy AND stayed protected — not wasted money
Quantify silent value: cover carried, hospitals in reach, zero co-pay safety net
Pre-empt the competitor cheap-quote temptation by anchoring on what full cover means

emails.purpose: Renewal path plus what's new / what's improved this year.
emails.subject: Renew early and lock this year's rate — plus what's new
emails.preview: Bigger network, same zero co-pay. Renew before your date.
keyPoints: Clear renewal CTA with early-renewal benefit (continuity, no waiting-period reset)
What's improved: expanded hospital network, product/app upgrades over the year
Stress continuity of benefits — lapsing resets hard-won waiting-period credit

emails.purpose: Expansion story — upgrade Standard → Platinum and the inflation-protection angle.
emails.subject: ₹10 lakh isn't what it was in 2025 — time to upgrade?
emails.preview: Platinum adds zero waiting period + 10% inflation protection each year.
keyPoints: Inflation reality: medical costs rise, ₹10L cover shrinks in real terms
Platinum upgrade: zero waiting period, 10% annual inflation protection, higher room cover
Family expansion prompt: add spouse, child, or parents to the floater at renewal

emails.purpose: Renewal reminder, remove friction, offer a human contact.
emails.subject: Your cover renews on [renewal date] — one tap to keep it
emails.preview: Don't let a great policy lapse over a missed date. Renew in minutes.
keyPoints: Concrete renewal date and exact premium — no surprises
Friction removal: saved payment, one-tap renewal, instant updated policy
Warn plainly: a lapse means losing accrued waiting-period and continuity benefits

emails.purpose: Celebrate the milestone and, for healthy accounts, make the expansion offer feel like a thank-you.
emails.subject: Happy 1 year with Acko, [First name] 🎉
emails.preview: Thanks for trusting us with your health cover. A little something inside.
keyPoints: Celebrate the anniversary and thank them for the trust
Surface member benefits and free check-up eligibility for the new year
Health-gated expansion nudge: add family or increase sum insured as a milestone step

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed policyholders and cold quote leads, and sunset non-responders to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Policy lapsed (non-renewal) OR 90 days inactivity with no opens/clicks/app logins. Two distinct branches with different messaging.
campaigns.segment: Lapsed customers (reference what they had + what's improved) vs dormant cold leads (fresh value + trust). Sunset both branches after the final email.
campaigns.rationale: Lapsed policyholders are uniquely valuable — they already trusted Acko once and a lapse often means losing accrued continuity benefits, which is a strong reason to return. Today these contacts are likely not systematically re-engaged. Win-back is quick to ship, recovers 5–15% of inactives, and cleaning the list protects sender reputation for the whole program.

emails.purpose: 'We miss you' — remind them coverage lapsed and what that exposes them to.
emails.subject: [First name], your health cover lapsed — you're currently unprotected
emails.preview: No fear-mongering, just facts: you have no active cover right now.
keyPoints: Plainly state the gap: the policy has lapsed and there's no active cover today
Reactivation is fast and fully online — minutes, no agent
Note what a lapse risks: losing accrued waiting-period and continuity credit

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt — what's improved since they left.
emails.subject: What's changed at Acko since you left
emails.preview: More hospitals, faster cashless, ZERO GST. Worth another look.
keyPoints: Expanded network (11,500+ hospitals) and faster cashless claims
ZERO GST = 18% instant saving vs typical premiums
Reinforce trust signals: 98% settlement, ₹400+ crore paid

emails.purpose: Personalised reason to return — a fresh quote and the free check-up incentive.
emails.subject: Here's a fresh quote — and a free health check-up to come back
emails.preview: Your new quote with ZERO GST, plus a free medical check-up.
keyPoints: Personalised fresh quote based on what they previously held
Free medical check-up for eligible returners as the win-back sweetener
One-tap path back to an instant digital policy

emails.purpose: Sunset — last chance to stay subscribed; clean the list.
emails.subject: Should we stop emailing you, [First name]?
emails.preview: We don't want to clutter your inbox. Tell us to stay — or we'll go quiet.
keyPoints: Honest sunset: if no response, we'll stop emailing to respect your inbox
One clear button to stay; easy unsubscribe per TRAI requirements
Suppress non-responders after send to protect deliverability

campaigns.name: Acko Health — Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long gap between purchase and renewal, building authority and felt value so the premium feels worth it even in a claim-free year — without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled monthly cadence. Suppress contacts in active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route recently-dormant to Win-Back (Campaign 4) instead.
campaigns.segment: All engaged policyholders and warm leads. Segment editions by life stage (young professional, family, senior-parent cover) via dynamic content blocks.
campaigns.rationale: The intake flags a near-total touchpoint gap between policy issuance and the renewal reminder — the exact window where no-claim customers start questioning value and competitors poach. A useful, mostly-value monthly newsletter (claim tips, health calendar, tax/80D timing, member benefits) keeps Acko present and trusted, directly supporting renewal. This is the always-on retention engine, built once as a repeatable template.

emails.purpose: Health-and-money tip edition; build the recurring 'one useful thing' habit.
emails.subject: 3 things to check in any health policy (yours included)
emails.preview: Sub-limits, waiting periods, room rent — the fine print that bites.
keyPoints: One useful idea: how to read policy fine print so you're never surprised
80/20 value-to-offer: mostly education, one soft member benefit
Recurring section: 'This month's coverage tip'

emails.purpose: Timely / seasonal edition tied to tax season (80D) or monsoon-season health.
emails.subject: Tax season: how your health cover cuts your bill under 80D
emails.preview: Your premium may save you more than you think come filing time.
keyPoints: Timely angle: 80D deduction for health premiums (informational, not tax advice)
Tie back to value: your cover protects health AND reduces tax
Soft nudge to keep cover active / consider increasing sum insured

emails.purpose: Mini customer-story edition reinforcing the trust narrative between claim events.
emails.subject: How one cashless claim saved this family ₹4.8 lakh
emails.preview: A real Acko claim, start to finish — no paperwork, no funds arranged.
keyPoints: Single real claim mini-case (IRDAI-compliant, with consent)
Reinforce cashless mechanics so members know the drill before they need it
Recurring proof point: 98% settlement keeps showing up quietly

emails.purpose: Member-benefit / how-to edition driving app engagement and feature awareness.
emails.subject: The Acko app feature most members forget they have
emails.preview: Digital ID card, hospital locator, one-tap claims — all in your pocket.
keyPoints: Spotlight an under-used app feature (locator, digital card, OPD/wellness benefit)
Drive a login — app engagement is a retention and activation signal
Single primary CTA; keep it to one useful idea per send

campaigns.name: Referral & Advocacy
campaigns.objective: Turn satisfied policyholders — especially those who've had a smooth claim — into a low-cost acquisition channel.
campaigns.trigger: Value moment: a settled cashless claim, a renewal, or a positive support interaction / high NPS. Also embedded into onboarding and renewal flows.
campaigns.segment: Healthy, engaged customers and post-claim 'promoters'. Prioritise members who just experienced the definitive 'aha' (a paid cashless claim).
campaigns.rationale: Acko has ~8 crore customers across products and no visible referral email program — a huge untapped channel flagged in the intake. Trust is the entire purchase barrier in insurance, and a referral from a friend who was actually paid is the most powerful trust signal available. Best embedded at the post-claim and renewal value moments rather than blasted, mirroring Dropbox's embedded-referral playbook.

emails.purpose: Trigger-based ask right after a positive moment — your claim went smoothly, share it.
emails.subject: Glad we had your back, [First name] — know someone who needs cover?
emails.preview: If Acko worked for you, it'll work for the people you care about.
keyPoints: Time the ask to a real value moment (settled claim, smooth renewal)
Frame around trust: a friend who was actually paid is the best recommendation
Make the share one tap — prefilled link, no friction

emails.purpose: Reminder with the reward framed for both sides.
emails.subject: Both of you win when you share Acko
emails.preview: A reward for you, peace of mind for them. Here's how it works.
keyPoints: Spell out the two-sided reward clearly (referrer + friend)
Reassure friends get the same self-serve, no-agent, ZERO-GST experience
Keep it simple: share link, friend buys, both benefit

emails.purpose: For non-referrers, pivot to a review / testimonial request to fuel social proof elsewhere.
emails.subject: Not ready to refer? Tell us how we did instead
emails.preview: A 30-second review helps the next person decide with confidence.
keyPoints: Lower-commitment ask for those who didn't refer
Reviews feed the social proof used in onboarding and nurture flows
Genuine reply-to for anything that fell short — close the feedback loop
